method_atom: A field-integral mapping that recasts a disordered many-body system with a continuous O(2) internal symmetry as a higher-dimensional classical statistical model carrying correlated, line-like (extended) quenched randomness, whose universality class is controlled by a discrete reflection symmetry of the couplings. The critical behavior is analyzed by renormalization-group flow and a double epsilon-expansion technique; generic symmetry-breaking perturbations are shown to be irrelevant so that the symmetric fixed point governs large-scale behavior, while an order-parameter stiffness that is positive when the symmetry is broken vanishes with an essential singularity as the symmetry is restored.
object_atom: The zero-temperature onset of superfluidity of interacting bosons in a quenched random medium (the dirty-boson problem), including the intervening Mott-insulating and Bose-glass phases and the role of particle-hole symmetry as tuned by the distribution of random site energies.
brief_reason: The symmetry-controlled RG / epsilon-expansion analysis of a disorder-mapped higher-dimensional classical model is the transferable machinery, whereas the interacting bosons, superfluid-insulator transition, and particle-hole symmetry are the domain-specific object.
